title: Panel Library
text: Panel Library Use this editor to define panels that can be used as toolbars for an application or form component. Panels contain buttons to execute structure editor functions and activate component operations. Operation buttons appear only in form panels and session panels; they are ignored in pop-up panels. Properties Description Function Character Attributes View Font Range Height Width Operation Glyph Name & Message Text How to Working with Panels Menu Options Table 1. Frequently-Used Menu Options for Panels Menu Option Action File > Open/New Create a new global object File > Open Open a new library. File > Duplicate Save a copy of the current panel under a different name. File > Delete Delete the current global object. File > Compile Compile the current panel. View >Zoom Field Edit the selected field in a separate dialog; useful when editing text blocks and code. View >Message Frame Display the message frame; useful after compiling the object. Go To <Global Object Type> Create or open a different type of global object, or library (properties). Related Links Panels Panels are global objects that are typically used to create toolbars for form components and application shells. They contain buttons to execute structure editor functions and activate component operations. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
